titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 613

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=918 status=reviewed modified=2022-11-01 16:47:55.58234+00 | translation moa=919 status=reviewed modified=2022-11-01 16:47:55.595084+00

p.cl: W . Rex Scott’ . Omnibus probis hominibus tocius terre sue salutem .

Sciatis me concessisse
deo . et sancto Cudberto . de Dunelmo . et monachis ibidem deo seruientibus  ut ipsi
et eorum seruientes per totam terram meam liberi fuit et quieti ab omni teloneo de propriis catallis et mercaturis eorum  sicut carta Regis M . fratris mei testatur et
confirmat . Et prohibeo . ne aliquis contra hanc libertatem eis infra terram meam
iniuriam faciat  super meam forisfacturam .

testibus Ricardo de Moreu' constabulario
philippo de ualon’ Camerario . Alano filio dapiferi .
William king of the Scots to all worthy men of his entire land greeting.

Know me to have granted to God and Saint Cuthbert of Durham and to the monks serving God in that place that they and their sergeands may be free throughout my whole land and quit from every toll on their own cattle and their merchandise, just as the charter of King Malcolm my brother bears witness and confirms. And I forbid that anyone do injustice to them against this liberty within my land, on pain of my forfeiture.
As witnesses, Richard de Morville the constable, Philip de Valognes the chamberlain, Alan son of the Steward.
